contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 60
- Description: Credential page: resolve pk-hash URL fragments via _cache/index.json
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta/pull/127
  
  Details:
  Mobile-discovered bug: clicking 'View your training records' on capoeira.agroverse.shop/practice.html landed on truesight.me/programs/tribomirim/credentials/#pk-wR9zU8JMnEz1 and showed 'Could not load this credential. The id pk-wR9zU8JMnEz1 may not exist yet' — even though that pk-hash is Gary's known DAO identity.
  
  Root cause: Capoeira practice page generates URL fragments of the form pk-<base64url> (derived from the practitioner's local keypair, since there's no DAO display identity before their first submission). renderCredential() in program-shell.js used the raw fragment directly as the cv-file lookup key, fetching _cache/cv/pk-wR9zU8JMnEz1.json — never exists. Cache is keyed by display slug (gary-teh.json), with pk_hash as a secondary field on each index.json member record. The renderer never consulted the index for resolution.
  
  Fix: new resolveCredentialSlug() step before the cv-fetch.
  - pk- prefixed fragments hit _cache/index.json to find the member where pk_hash === fragment, use that record's slug.
  - Direct slug fragments pass through unchanged (preserves existing QR scan + in-site link behavior).
  - pk-hash not in the index (truly-new practitioner whose first event hasn't been picked up by the 6h cache cron) → friendlier 'Your training record is being generated' placeholder instead of the 404 error. The Capoeira practice page already promises this UX in a comment; this brings the rendering side into line.
  - Index fetch failure → fall back to raw fragment as last-ditch.
  
  Cost: one extra fetch per credential page view (the index), only for pk-hash fragments. The index is already cacheable + small.
  
  Promotion to prod: cherry-picked the fix commit onto truesight_me_prod main rather than 'gh repo sync --force' — that would have re-overwritten the CNAME file the same way it did this morning (the bug captured in the 2026-05-19 CONTEXT_UPDATES entry). Cherry-pick touches only js/program-shell.js; CNAME and the protect-cname guardrail workflow stayed put. Verified post-push: prod's CNAME still 'truesight.me'.
  
  PR: TrueSightDAO/truesight_me_beta#127 (squash-merged fd2e296); prod promotion via cherry-pick ce216ee.
- Contributor(s): Gary Teh
- TDG Issued: 100.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
